Title: Autoactive CNGC15 enhances root endosymbiosis in legume and wheat

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Dominant gain-of-function mutation P98S autoactivates nuclear Ca2+ oscillations, enhances nodulation, AM colonization, and nutrient acquisition, and promotes flavonoid accumulation through low-frequency Ca2+ signaling.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in roots; expression level in roots was not altered by GOF mutation.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root infection zone used for transcriptomics; channel acts in root hair nuclei and symbiotic roots.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TILLING mutant identification; backcross segregation; nuclear Ca2+ imaging with YC3.6; nodulation assay; AM colonization assay; RNA-seq; metabolite profiling; field transfer concept validated in wheat ortholog
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nod factor; rhizobial inoculation with Sinorhizobium meliloti 2011; AM inoculation with Rhizophagus irregularis; nitrate (3 mM KNO3)
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No growth penalty detected in root/shoot dry weight without symbionts; low-frequency oscillation alone cannot restore epidermal infection in dmi1 background.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoids enhance root endosymbiosis and nutrient acquisition.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mutation P98S in helix S1 is dominant GOF; autoactivity bypasses DMI1 requirement for channel opening but not for high-frequency pacing. The paper’s key mechanistic discovery centers on frequency-encoded Ca2+ signaling.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CNGC15a P98S induces spontaneous low-frequency nuclear Ca2+ oscillations. 2. This enhances rhizobial infection, nodulation, AM colonization and leaf N:C ratio. 3. Downstream transcriptomic and metabolomic changes show modulation of phenylpropanoid pathways and increased flavonoids that benefit symbiosis.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Dominant gain-of-function mutation P104S autoactivates low-frequency nuclear Ca2+ oscillations, enhances nodulation and AM colonization, and is associated with increased root flavonoid abundance and nutrient acquisition.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root-expressed; GOF mutation does not alter root expression level.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functions in root hair nuclei and symbiotic roots.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TILLING mutant identification; nuclear Ca2+ imaging; nodulation assay; AM colonization assay; localization assay; RNA-seq and metabolomics in cngc15cGOF roots
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nod factor; rhizobial inoculation with Sinorhizobium meliloti 2011; AM inoculation with Rhizophagus irregularis
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low-frequency oscillation is insufficient for full epidermal infection without DMI1 pacing.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoids promote nodulation and AM colonization, improving nutrient uptake.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: P104S corresponds to the conserved S1 proline mutation analogous to CNGC15a P98S; cngc15cGOF was the main line used for metabolomics.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CNGC15c P104S autoactivates low-frequency nuclear Ca2+ oscillations. 2. The mutant shows increased nodulation, AM colonization and leaf/shoot nutrient-related benefits. 3. Metabolomics directly linked cngc15cGOF to elevated naringenin, liquiritigenin and isoliquiritigenin.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: P111S gain-of-function allele enhances AM colonization in greenhouse and field conditions and increases flag leaf N:C ratio in field-grown wheat; associated with increased root flavonoids such as liquiritigenin/retusin.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Acts in roots during AM interaction.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TILLING allele identification; greenhouse AM assay; field trial; near-isogenic BC3F2 validation; targeted metabolite analysis
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: AM inoculation with Rhizophagus irregularis; field soil with residual nitrate and phosphate
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolite analysis by LC-MS in wheat roots
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoids support AM colonization and nutrient acquisition.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is the translational crop ortholog carrying the conserved helix-1 GOF mutation. Applied species equals source species here.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The P111S mutation in TaCNGC15a-D1 is sufficient to enhance AM colonization in wheat. 2. The effect persists in field soil containing residual fertilizer nutrients. 3. Enhanced colonization correlates with increased flag leaf N:C ratio and elevated flavonoid-related metabolites.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: P111L gain-of-function allele enhances AM colonization in greenhouse and field conditions in tetraploid wheat, supporting improved nutrient-acquisition symbiosis.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Acts in roots during AM interaction.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TILLING allele identification; greenhouse AM assay; field trial; near-isogenic BC3F2 validation
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: AM inoculation with Rhizophagus irregularis; field soil with residual nitrate and phosphate
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoids support AM colonization and nutrient acquisition.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: P111L was predicted to confer the same structural effect as P111S. Evidence focused on symbiosis phenotype rather than metabolite quantification.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TtCNGC15a-A1 P111L enhances AM colonization under greenhouse conditions. 2. The phenotype is reproduced in field-grown near-isogenic lines. 3. This supports transferability of the CNGC15GOF trait to cereal crops.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulated in cngc15aGOF in a DMI3/CCaMK-dependent manner, consistent with reduced allocation toward dirigent-dependent phenylpropanoid products and favoring flavonoid accumulation.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root expression responsive to Nod factor and cngc15aGOF status.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in cngc15aGOF roots, validated by qRT-PCR.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; dmi3-1 genetic dependency test
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phenolic products contribute to plant phenylpropanoid metabolism; relative downregulation may favor flavonoid-promoting symbiotic outcomes.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: DIR proteins are presented as pathway competitors/modulators rather than direct nutrient metabolites. Included because the paper experimentally validates DIR23 as part of the metabolic mechanism downstream of CNGC15GOF.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DIR23 is downregulated in cngc15GOF roots. 2. This regulation depends on the Ca2+ decoder DMI3/CCaMK. 3. Reduced DIR expression is interpreted as favoring flavonoid production over alternative phenylpropanoid outputs.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulated in cngc15aGOF in a DMI3/CCaMK-dependent manner, supporting reprogramming of phenylpropanoid branch allocation associated with higher flavonoid production.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root expression responsive to Nod factor and cngc15aGOF status.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in cngc15aGOF roots, validated by qRT-PCR.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; dmi3-1 genetic dependency test
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phenylpropanoid branch product control; relative repression may permit increased flavonoid-associated symbiotic benefit.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because qRT-PCR validation in Results supports this gene as part of the downstream metabolic branch modulation caused by CNGC15GOF.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DIR44 is downregulated in cngc15GOF roots. 2. Its altered expression depends on DMI3/CCaMK. 3. The data support branch reprogramming of phenylpropanoid metabolism toward flavonoid accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flavonoid production and thereby enhances nodulation and arbuscular mycorrhizal colonization; CHS silencing reduces both symbioses.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry step into flavonoid branch from phenylpropanoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies core precursor pool for downstream flavonoids that promote endosymbiosis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as key control point for flavonoid-dependent symbiosis in this study
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Several CHS genes were upregulated in cngc15GOF after Nod factor treatment and showed nitrate insensitivity; CHS-1B upregulated during later nodulation stages.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nod factor treatment; rhizobial inoculation with Sinorhizobium meliloti 2011; arbuscular mycorrhizal colonization; nitrate (3 mM KNO3)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; RNAi silencing in composite roots; nodulation assay; AM colonization assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful pathway target to boost flavonoid-mediated symbiosis, but broad pleiotropic effects on phenylpropanoid metabolism should be considered.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoids stimulate rhizobial Nod factor production and promote AM spore germination/colonization, improving nutrient acquisition.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CHS is not the main discovery gene of the paper but is experimentally validated in Results as a causal flavonoid-pathway enzyme contributing to enhanced symbiosis downstream of low-frequency nuclear Ca2+ oscillations.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS genes were over-represented among phenylpropanoid genes primed/upregulated in cngc15GOF. 2. CHS-1B expression increased during nodulation and was stronger in cngc15aGOF backgrounds. 3. RNAiCHS reduced nodulation and AM colonization, supporting a causal role of flavonoid biosynthesis in endosymbiosis enhancement.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Low-frequency Ca2+ oscillation in cngc15GOF is associated with upregulation of PAL genes, contributing to enhanced phenylpropanoid/flavonoid pathway activity.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Phenylalanine ammonia lyase genes were over-represented among Nod factor-primed genes in cngc15GOF.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoids promote root endosymbioses and nutrient acquisition.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is explicitly highlighted in Results as part of the phenylpropanoid pathway module modulated downstream of CNGC15GOF, but no direct single-gene functional test was presented here.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAL genes were enriched among phenylpropanoid genes primed by low-frequency Ca2+ oscillations. 2. Their induction is associated with enhanced flavonoid accumulation and symbiosis promotion. 3. Evidence is transcriptomic rather than direct reverse-genetic validation.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Low-frequency Ca2+ oscillation in cngc15GOF is associated with upregulation of 4CL genes, supporting enhanced flux into phenylpropanoid/flavonoid metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Produces immediate substrate for CHS and downstream flavonoid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: 4CL genes were over-represented among phenylpropanoid genes primed in cngc15GOF after Nod factor treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoids promote root endosymbiotic interactions and nutrient acquisition.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct functional genetics for 4CL in this paper; evidence is pathway-level transcriptomic enrichment.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 4CL genes were highlighted among central phenylpropanoid enzymes primed in cngc15GOF. 2. Their induction supports increased flavonoid biosynthesis. 3. Evidence is based on transcriptome association with metabolite and symbiosis phenotypes.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required to sustain spontaneous low-frequency nuclear Ca2+ oscillations generated by CNGC15GOF; thereby indirectly supports flavonoid pathway modulation and enhanced endosymbiosis.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Negative-feedback gating of CNGC15 channel activity
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirect; modulates CNGC15-dependent Ca2+ oscillatory signaling that alters phenylpropanoid/flavonoid gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conditionally decisive because silencing CaM2 impaired spontaneous Ca2+ oscillations in cngc15aGOF/dmi1 background, linking it to downstream metabolic rewiring.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Signal decoding/gating upstream of flavonoid biosynthesis and symbiosis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nod factor context; dmi1 mutant background
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi silencing combined with nuclear Ca2+ imaging
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not a transcription factor, CaM2 is a core signaling regulator experimentally tested in Results for control of CNGC15 oscillation dynamics.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CaM2-mediated gating sustains spontaneous oscillatory behavior of autoactive CNGC15. 2. Silencing CaM2 impairs spontaneous nuclear Ca2+ oscillations. 3. It functions as a feedback regulator upstream of phenylpropanoid/flavonoid reprogramming.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as pacemaker specifying high-frequency nuclear Ca2+ oscillations required for full symbiotic epidermal infection; absence shifts signaling capacity so CNGC15GOF only drives low-frequency oscillations linked to phenylpropanoid/flavonoid modulation.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Activates and frequency-controls CNGC15-dependent nuclear Ca2+ oscillations
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirectly controls symbiotic and phenylpropanoid gene expression through Ca2+ oscillation frequency
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Ca2+ binding to C-terminal pockets; Nod factor signaling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Decisive controller of oscillation frequency, which determines whether endosymbiosis programs or flavonoid-modulatory programs are engaged.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Nuclear envelope ion channel; BKca-like C-terminal regulatory domain
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Signal control upstream of symbiosis-associated phenylpropanoid/flavonoid metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive feedback from Ca2+ onto DMI1 pacemaker activity
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout mutant; double mutant with cngc15GOF; Ca2+ imaging; nodulation assay; mutant complementation with DMI1TVGYG and Ca2+-binding-site variants
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of DMI1 abolishes proper high-frequency oscillation and epidermal infection
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: DMI1 is central to signaling but not itself shown to directly regulate flavonoid genes; included because the paper establishes Ca2+ frequency encoding that underlies the metabolic phenotype.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DMI1 is required to activate CNGC15 in WT and set high-frequency nuclear Ca2+ oscillations. 2. CNGC15GOF bypasses DMI1 for opening but not for high-frequency pacing. 3. Frequency control by DMI1 explains differential activation of symbiotic versus phenylpropanoid/flavonoid programs.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for part of the transcriptional reprogramming downstream of low-frequency nuclear Ca2+ oscillations, including CCaMK-dependent downregulation of dirigent genes and priming of phenylpropanoid pathways.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Decoding nuclear Ca2+ oscillations into downstream gene-expression outputs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Controls expression of CCaMK-dependent phenylpropanoid-related genes such as DIRs and symbiosis-responsive gene modules
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: DIR23; DIR44; broader CCaMK-dependent gene set
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important decoder linking oscillation frequency to metabolic transcriptional outputs, though not sole determinant of final flavonoid accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Signal decoding upstream of phenylpropanoid/flavonoid pathway transcription
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic backcross with dmi3-1; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Known symbiosis decoder; here it is explicitly used to test whether cngc15GOF transcriptomic effects depend on canonical Ca2+ decoding.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. cngc15GOF transcriptomic effects include a CCaMK-dependent component. 2. dmi3-1 background helped show that low-frequency Ca2+ signaling does not activate canonical early nodulation genes but does affect phenylpropanoid-related genes. 3. DMI3 therefore links oscillatory signals to metabolic pathway reprogramming.
